notes: REFUTED OBSERVABLE (agent limitation, NOT a daemon gap): the scenario wants a mid-session model CHANGE (turn 1 model A, turn 2 model B, B latest at session end). The live recordings prove gemini-cli does NOT produce this under AI-Studio API-key auth: `/model set gemini-2.5-flash` is ACCEPTED mid-session (the TUI prints `Model set to gemini-2.5-flash`, config.setModel runs), but the recorded transcript `model` field does NOT flip - BOTH turns record `gemini-3.5-flash`. Settled across TWO recordings (3s and 8s post-slash gaps; B=gemini-2.5-flash IS listed by the key's models endpoint, so this is not an unavailable-model fallback) - see recordings/2026-06-12-11-11-59. The daemon is CORRECT: it faithfully reports the only model the transcript carries; there is no model-change signal to surface. So observations.model asserts the ACTUALLY-recorded id (gemini-3.5-flash), not the unmet target B. WHAT THIS RECORDING DOES ASSERT (all real + passing): two clean ready->working->ready turns on one rollout session, a no-flicker /model-set window (state stays ready, the inline slash does no LLM round-trip and writes no turn message), distinct per-turn token deltas (two real requests), and cost+tokens nonzero with agent=gemini-cli. To capture a TRUE model switch a future recording needs an auth/model combination where gemini-cli actually flips the recorded per-turn model. The state machinery (per-turn model attribution, MergeMetrics latest-wins, no-flicker switch window) remains daemon=full - it simply has no differing input to promote here. Two distinct turns separated by `/model set gemini-2.5-flash` (inline-argument form, a plain slash step - NOT the arrow-key picker).

text: Turn 1 user prompt -> working (model recorded as gemini-3.5-flash)
Turn 1 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = gemini-3.5-flash

/model set slash runs locally (no LLM round-trip, no turn message); state stays ready, no working flicker. NB: the switch is accepted by the CLI but does not change the recorded per-turn model under API-key auth (see meta.notes).
Turn 2 user prompt -> working (model still recorded as gemini-3.5-flash; the requested switch to gemini-2.5-flash did not flip the transcript model)
Turn 2 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = gemini-3.5-flash; TotalTokens after T2 > after T1 (two real requests)
